channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Gunmen kill 7 people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Gunmen fatally shot at least seven people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir and injured at least five others, officials said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel apologizes for strike that killed 3 Lebanese soldiers
item.description: The Israeli military apologized Monday for a strike that killed three Lebanese soldiers in southern Lebanon the previous day, saying it is not battling the country's military and its soldiers believed they were targeting a vehicle belonging to the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar brings winds and heavy rains to eastern Cuba
item.description: Tropical Storm Oscar was moving across eastern Cuba after making landfall as a Category 1 hurricane on the island already beleaguered by a massive power outage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel's wars are expensive. Paying the bill could force tough choices
item.description: On top of the grievous toll in human life and misery, Israel's war against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups has been expensive, and the painfully high financial costs are raising concerns about the long-term effect of the fighting on the country's economy.
item.dc:creator: DAVID McHUGH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cubans struggle with an extended power outage and a new tropical storm
item.description: Many Cubans waited in anguish and some took to the streets in protest as widespread blackouts stretched into their third day.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodriguez And Milexsy Duran, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Environmental delegates gather in Colombia for a conference on dwindling global biodiversity
item.description: As the world grapples with plummeting levels of biodiversity, Colombia is hosting two weeks of meetings for the United Nations Biodiversity Conference.
item.dc:creator: Steven Grattan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: German authorities arrest Libyan man suspected of planning attack on Israeli Embassy
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German authorities said Sunday that they have arrested a Libyan national with suspected ties to the extremist Islamic State group who was allegedly planning a firearms attack on the Israeli Embassy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the electrical grid failure that plunged Cuba into darkness
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Millions of people in Cuba were left without electricity for two days after the nation's energy grid went down when one of the island's major power plants failed. The widespread blackout that swept across the county was the worst in years.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine’s ‘victory plan’ receives mixed reactions from Western allies
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s plan to end Ukraine’s nearly three-year war with Russia has received mixed reactions from Western allies so far.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reddit user discovers mysterious 'doorway' in Antarctica
item.description: "Monsters Inc." bedroom doors, and an underground bunker were more suggested explanations, some in jest others not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Children greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla outside a Sydney church
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — King Charles III and Queen Camilla were greeted by children Sunday outside a Sydney church in their first public appearance of their Australian visit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuba hit by second blackout after grid collapse
item.description: Cubans experienced their second black out wave Saturday after government officials said they were working to restore electrical service to hospitals and crucial service centers after a grid failure on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Japan's governing party headquarters is attacked with firebombs and suspect is arrested
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s governing party in Tokyo on Saturday, then crashed his car into the fencing of the prime minister's residence, police said. There were no reports of injuries.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Video published by Ukraine allegedly shows North Korean soldiers in Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A video purporting to show dozens of North Korean recruits lining up to collect Russian military fatigues has been released by Ukrainian officials, who say it shows the introduction of troops sent by Pyongyang into the conflict.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Strike on Netanyahu home 'a signal' Iran proxies not incapacitated, expert says
item.description: A recent drone strike targeting the home of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending "a signal" that Iran-backed militant groups are not "incapacitated," despite losing senior leadership in recent weeks, according to a Middle East expert. Daniel Levy, the president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: France vows support for Ukraine's plan to end Russian invasion
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — France's foreign minister pledged his support for Ukraine’s plan for ending the war with Russia, telling reporters in Kyiv on Saturday that he would work with Ukrainian officials to secure other nations’ backing for the proposal.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah fires rocket salvo at northern Israel, Iranian leader vows 'Axis of Resistance' will continue
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said at least 180 projectiles were fired into Israel from Lebanon as Hezbollah ramps up its attacks on the Jewish state with backing from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says drone launched toward Netanyahu's home from Lebanon
item.description: The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said early Saturday that a drone was launched from Lebanon toward Netanyahu's home in Caesarea. Netanyahu and his wife were not home at the time of the incident and no casualties were reported, according to The Associated Press (AP).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu’s defiance of Biden-Harris Rafah invasion threats led to elimination of Sinwar, experts say
item.description: Netanyahu dismissed an international campaign to prevent Israel from seizing the Hamas bastion of Rafah in Gaza, where Israel killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea troops readying for Ukraine combat: South Korea
item.description: South Korea said it's used facial recognition AI tech to identify North Korean officers in eastern Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ju-min Park, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: South Korea's spy agency said North Korea has sent troops to support Russia's war in Ukraine, transferring 1,500 North Korean special operation forces to the Russian city of Vladivostok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: IDF video: Israeli tank fires at building where Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was barricaded before being killed
item.description: A building where Hamas chief Yahya Sinwar had been barricaded was targeted by Israeli tank fire before the terror leader was killed on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the Uluburun shipwreck was discovered accidentally by a sponge diver
item.description: The Uluburun shipwreck was accidently discovered by a sponge diver in 1982. Following the discovery, excavation of the area took archaeologists a decade to complete.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas admits 'painful, distressing' losses after Israeli video shows terrorist Sinwar moments before his death
item.description: Hamas is admitting to suffering "very painful and distressing" losses following the killing of leader Yahya Sinwar as IDF footage reveals his final moments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Former Mexican public security chief gets more than 38 years, $2M fine for taking cartel bribes
item.description: Mexico’s ex-public security secretary Genaro García Luna was sentenced to more than 38 years in prison and a $2 million fine for taking millions in bribes to protect the Sinaloa cartel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a symbolic display of anger, North Korea on Tuesday blew up the northern sections of unused road and rail routes that once linked it with South Korea, with the rivals exchanging threats days after the North claimed that the South flew drones over its capital Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation
